Title: IWS - Verify device appears in Peripheral Manager discovery
2. Create a new design.
3. IWS device is powered on and connected to the same network as the Q-SYS Core.
Preconditions: 1. Open Menu Bar > Tools > Peripheral Manager (or Configurator).

Title: 2. Initiate device discovery / scan.
3. Review the discovered devices list.
Preconditions: 1. The IWS device appears in the discovered devices list.

Title: 2. The device shows its correct model identifier, IP address, and status.
3. The device is listed as "Not Commissioned" or equivalent.
IWS - Commission a discovered IWS device
2. Create a new design.
3. IWS device is discovered in Peripheral Manager but not yet commissioned.
2. Select the discovered IWS device.
3. Enter the device credentials (username/password).
4. Click the commission/add button.
Preconditions: 1. The device status changes from "Not Commissioned" to "Connected" or "OK".

Title: 2. The device appears in the design inventory as an assigned peripheral.
3. Sensor data begins flowing to the component.
IWS - Commission with incorrect credentials
2. Create a new design.
3. IWS device is discovered in Peripheral Manager but not yet commissioned.
2. Select the discovered IWS device.
3. Enter incorrect credentials (wrong username or password).
4. Click the commission/add button.
Preconditions: 1. A clear error message is displayed indicating authentication failure.

Title: 2. The device remains in "Not Commissioned" state.
3. No crash or unhandled exception occurs.
IWS - Commission with empty credentials
2. Create a new design.
3. IWS device is discovered in Peripheral Manager but not yet commissioned.
2. Select the discovered IWS device.
3. Leave credential fields empty.
4. Click the commission/add button.
Preconditions: 1. A validation message indicates that credentials are required.

Title: 2. The device is not commissioned.
3. The UI does not allow proceeding without credentials.
IWS - Decommission a commissioned device
2. Create a new design.
3. IWS device is commissioned and connected.
2. Select the commissioned IWS device.
3. Click the decommission/remove button.
4. Confirm the action if prompted.
Preconditions: 1. The device status changes to "Not Commissioned" or is removed from the assigned list.

Title: 2. Sensor data stops flowing to the component.
3. The component on the canvas shows a disconnected/unassigned state.
IWS - Verify firmware version displayed
2. Create a new design.
3. IWS device is commissioned and connected.
2. Select the commissioned IWS device.
3. Locate the firmware version information.
Preconditions: 1. The current firmware version of the IWS device is displayed.

Title: 2. The version string is in a recognizable format.
IWS - Firmware update when update is available
2. Create a new design.
3. IWS device is commissioned and connected.
4. A firmware update is available for the IWS device.
2. Select the commissioned IWS device.
3. Locate the firmware update option.
4. Initiate the firmware update.
5. Wait for the update to complete.
Preconditions: 1. A progress indicator shows during the firmware update.

Title: 2. The update completes successfully.
3. The firmware version updates to the new version.
4. The device reconnects after the update without manual intervention.
IWS - Firmware update when device is already up to date
2. Create a new design.
3. IWS device is commissioned, connected, and running the latest firmware.
2. Select the commissioned IWS device.
3. Check for firmware update availability.
Preconditions: 1. The UI indicates that the device firmware is already up to date.

Title: 2. No update is offered or initiated.
IWS - Verify device status indicators in Peripheral Manager
2. Create a new design.
3. IWS device is commissioned and connected.
2. Review the status column for the IWS device.
3. Note the status indicator (icon, color, text).
Preconditions: 1. The status indicator shows a healthy/connected state (e.g., green icon, "OK" text).

Title: 2. Device name, model, IP address, and firmware version are all visible.
IWS - Verify Peripheral Manager status when device goes offline
2. Create a new design.
3. IWS device is commissioned and connected.
2. Disconnect the IWS device from the network.
3. Wait for the status to update.
4. Observe the device status.
Preconditions: 1. The device status changes to a fault/disconnected state (e.g., red icon, "Fault" or "Not Present" text).

Title: 2. The status update occurs within a reasonable timeout period.
IWS - Verify Peripheral Manager status when device comes back online
2. Create a new design.
3. IWS device is commissioned but currently disconnected from network.
2. Reconnect the IWS device to the network.
3. Wait for the status to update.
4. Observe the device status.
Preconditions: 1. The device status returns to healthy/connected state.

Title: 2. No manual re-commissioning is required.
3. Sensor data resumes flowing.
IWS - Assign IWS device to component in design
2. Create a new design.
3. Add an IWS component to the canvas.
4. IWS device is discovered but not assigned to any component.
2. Select the IWS device.
3. Assign it to the IWS component in the design (e.g., via dropdown or drag).
Preconditions: 1. The device is assigned to the IWS component.

Title: 2. The component on the canvas reflects the connected state.
3. Sensor data begins appearing in Floating Window "Intelligent Workspace Sensor".
IWS - Reassign IWS device to a different component
2. Create a new design.
3. Add two IWS components to the canvas.
4. IWS device is assigned to the first component.
2. Select the IWS device currently assigned to "IWS 1".
3. Reassign it to "IWS 2".
Preconditions: 1. The device is removed from "IWS 1" assignment.

Title: 2. The device is now assigned to "IWS 2".
3. "IWS 1" shows unassigned/disconnected state.
4. "IWS 2" shows connected state and receives sensor data.
IWS - Verify device discovery with multiple IWS devices
2. Create a new design.
3. Multiple IWS devices (at least 2) are powered on and connected to the network.
2. Initiate device discovery.
3. Review the discovered devices list.
Preconditions: 1. All IWS devices on the network are discovered and listed.

Title: 2. Each device shows a unique identifier (IP address, serial number, or MAC address).
3. Devices can be individually selected and managed.
IWS - Verify Peripheral Manager with device password change
2. Create a new design.
3. IWS device is commissioned and connected with known credentials.
Preconditions: 1. Change the IWS device password via the device web interface or other management tool.

Title: 2. Return to Peripheral Manager in Q-SYS Designer.
3. Observe the device status after the password change.
Preconditions: 1. The device status changes to indicate an authentication error or fault.

Title: 2. A clear message indicates the credentials are no longer valid.
3. Re-entering the new credentials restores the connection.
